content.problemStatement:
Ada has a farm in the shape of a convex polygon. For simplicity, the vertices of the polygon are numbered 111 through NNN in clockwise order.

There are many cows living on the farm. Cows fight often, so Ada wants to divide the farm into separate regions using fences to prevent accidents. Each fence can be considered a line segment.

Ada is worried about the free space available for each cow, so she needs to know the area of the region inhabited by a particular cow sometimes. As her apprentice, your task is to perform QQQ queries. There are two types of queries:


1  u  v1\;u\;v1uv: Build a fence between vertices uuu and vvv. It is guaranteed that this fence does not intersect any previously built fence (but they may have common endpoints).
2  x  y2\;x\;y2xy: Find the area of the region inhabited by a cow at position (x,y)(x, y)(x,y). Sometimes, the point may lie on a fence (the cow is trying to cross the fence); it may also be outside the farm or on its border (the cow is escaping!). In these cases, the area is 000.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting the coordinates of the iii-th vertex.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains three space-separated integers describing a query in the above format.
content.outputFormat: For each query of the second type, print a single line containing one real number with exactly one decimal place — the area of the given region.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

The farm is a hexagon as depicted in the picture above. The queries are as follows:

Query on point G\mathsf{G}G, the answer is the area of the hexagon.
Add fence AD\mathsf{AD}AD, the farm is split into two regions.
Again a query on point G\mathsf{G}G, the answer is the area of region ABCD\mathsf{ABCD}ABCD.
Add fence DF\mathsf{DF}DF, the farm is split into three regions in total.
Query on point H\mathsf{H}H, the answer is the area of region DEF\mathsf{DEF}DEF.
